terms.definition: Verbal direction asking the patient to suspend respiration during image acquisition.
terms.exampleSentence: Please follow my breath-hold instructions carefully so we get clear images of your chest.
terms.contextNotes: Rehearse before scanning; reduces motion artefact.

terms.definition: Pre-procedure questioning to identify prior reactions to iodinated or gadolinium contrast.
terms.exampleSentence: Contrast allergy screening should be completed and documented before any contrast administration.
terms.contextNotes: Document outcome and any premedication plan.

terms.definition: Mandatory verification of pregnancy status before ionising radiation exposure.
terms.exampleSentence: Pregnancy check confirmed her LMP was 14 days ago, within the 28-day rule for elective imaging.
terms.contextNotes: Apply 28-day rule for females of childbearing age.

terms.definition: Documented patient agreement after explanation of risks, benefits and alternatives.
terms.exampleSentence: Informed consent for IV contrast was obtained after explaining the risk of allergic reaction.
terms.contextNotes: Document risks discussed and questions answered.

terms.definition: Reporting phrase indicating no urgent or new pathology identified on imaging.
terms.exampleSentence: Conclusion: no acute abnormality demonstrated on this CT head examination.
terms.contextNotes: Standard negative reporting phrase; specify region.

terms.definition: Reporting shorthand referencing findings already described earlier in the report.
terms.exampleSentence: Impression: as above; recommend MRI for further characterisation.
terms.contextNotes: Use sparingly; avoid in time-critical impressions.

terms.definition: Reporting recommendation that imaging findings be interpreted with clinical context.
terms.exampleSentence: Subtle ground-glass opacity in the right lower lobe; correlate clinically.
terms.contextNotes: Common closing recommendation in radiology reports.

terms.definition: Documented clinical reason confirming the imaging study benefit outweighs the risk.
terms.exampleSentence: Request justification was completed by the radiologist before authorising the CT.
terms.contextNotes: IRMER 2017 requires justified, optimised exposure.

terms.definition: Registered clinician entitled to refer a patient for a medical exposure under IRMER 2017.
terms.exampleSentence: The IRMER referrer is responsible for providing sufficient clinical information to justify the request.
terms.contextNotes: Distinct IRMER roles: referrer, practitioner, operator.

terms.definition: The doctor or healthcare professional initiating the imaging request.
terms.exampleSentence: The requesting clinician was contacted directly to discuss the unexpected critical finding.
terms.contextNotes: Direct verbal communication for critical findings.

terms.definition: Multidisciplinary team meeting where complex cases are reviewed by clinicians and radiologists.
terms.exampleSentence: Her CT findings will be discussed at the lung MDT meeting on Thursday.
terms.contextNotes: Imaging usually presented by a designated radiologist.

terms.definition: Formal written interpretation of an imaging study issued by a radiologist.
terms.exampleSentence: The radiology report will be available on the electronic record within 24 hours.
terms.contextNotes: Structured: clinical info, technique, findings, impression.

terms.definition: Real-time radiology reporting at the time of acquisition, typically for emergency or trauma cases.
terms.exampleSentence: Hot reporting is provided overnight for all major trauma CT studies.
terms.contextNotes: Used in ED and trauma; immediate verbal feedback common.

terms.definition: Delayed radiology reporting performed after the patient has left the department.
terms.exampleSentence: Routine outpatient chest radiographs are processed via cold reporting within 48 hours.
terms.contextNotes: Acceptable for non-urgent outpatient work.

terms.definition: Independent interpretation of the same imaging study by two reporters to reduce error.
terms.exampleSentence: All screening mammograms undergo double reading by two consultant radiologists.
terms.contextNotes: Standard for breast screening; reduces miss rate.

terms.definition: Provisional radiology interpretation issued before formal verification.
terms.exampleSentence: A preliminary report has been issued; the verified report will follow within an hour.
terms.contextNotes: Common in ED out-of-hours; formal report supersedes.

terms.definition: Imaging result requiring immediate communication to the requesting clinician.
terms.exampleSentence: Pulmonary embolism is a critical finding and must be communicated by phone immediately.
terms.contextNotes: Requires documented closed-loop communication.

terms.definition: Unexpected abnormality discovered on imaging unrelated to the clinical question.
terms.exampleSentence: An incidental adrenal lesion was noted; further characterisation is recommended.
terms.contextNotes: Recommend appropriate follow-up per local guidelines.

terms.definition: Verbal exchange where the receiver repeats back information to confirm understanding.
terms.exampleSentence: Closed-loop communication was used to confirm receipt of the critical finding.
terms.contextNotes: Mandatory for critical/unexpected findings.

terms.definition: Structured handover tool: Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation.
terms.exampleSentence: Use SBAR when phoning the on-call radiologist about the deteriorating patient.
terms.contextNotes: Endorsed by NHS and WHO for clinical handover.

terms.definition: Departmental review meeting where reporting errors and learning points are discussed.
terms.exampleSentence: The case will be presented anonymously at the next radiology discrepancy meeting.
terms.contextNotes: RCR-recommended quality improvement forum.

terms.definition: Final radiology report electronically signed off by the responsible radiologist.
terms.exampleSentence: The verified report supersedes the earlier preliminary interpretation.
terms.contextNotes: Stops the clock on reporting turnaround time.

terms.definition: A trained third party present during intimate examinations to safeguard patient and clinician.
terms.exampleSentence: A chaperone was offered and accepted before the transvaginal ultrasound was performed.
terms.contextNotes: Document offer, name, and outcome in the record.

terms.definition: Verification of three patient identifiers immediately before any examination.
terms.exampleSentence: Patient identification check confirmed name, date of birth, and NHS number prior to the scan.
terms.contextNotes: Use three identifiers; ask, don't tell.

terms.definition: Interval between completion of imaging and issue of the verified radiology report.
terms.exampleSentence: Report turnaround time for inpatient CT studies should not exceed 24 hours.
terms.contextNotes: Key departmental KPI; benchmarked against RCR standards.
